content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be reconfigured to act as a dynamic "surprise archive" that prunes and prioritizes curiosity signals for intrinsic reinforcement learning, solving the long-standing problem of distracting novelty from noisy task environments. This framework reframes curiosity-driven exploration not as a constant reward signal, but as an active learning process that queries the agent's own memory to identify which unexpected experiences are worth exploring further.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
